name: Ash House (Fort de la Frèniere)
original_story: The Silent Fort: A suspenseful narrative focused on the final year of operations, depicting the anxiety of traders during a lone winter at a fort "too open to the incursions of the Sioux Indians". The story ends with the hasty decision to desert the post just before David Thompson’s 1797 arrival found it empty.

name: Bacon Ridge
original_story: The Fringe Dwellers: A social drama exploring the lives of a family at Bacon Ridge, navigating the cultural and legal gap between the neighboring reserve and the provincial government while harvesting maple syrup.

name: Baie St. Paul (Saulteaux Village)
original_story: Graves in the Current: A haunting historical piece set during the catastrophic 1882 flood. As the cemetery slides into the rising Assiniboine, families are forced to make a difficult trek to North Dakota with their parish priest, Cyrille Saint-Pierre, to seek a new beginning.

name: Balsam Bay
original_story: Guardians of the Bay: A narrative centered on the community’s efforts to maintain their unique identity and their sacred free burial grounds as the surrounding area begins to transform into a popular tourist destination for outsiders.

name: Barrows (Baden, Powell, National Mills and Red Deer Lake)
original_story: The Last Sawmill: A historical drama set in 1926 following a Métis family in Barrows as the Red Deer Lumber Company mill whistles blow for the final time. The story explores their struggle to maintain their deep connection to the Porcupine Forest while the economic engine of their town is dismantled, forcing a choice between the land they have occupied since the 1700s and seeking labor in the cities to the south.

name: Bas de la Rivière (Fort Alexander)
original_story: The Provisioning Brigade: A historical account set during a winter of starvation in the early Red River Settlement. The narrative follows the Métis staff at Bas de la Rivière as they manage a massive ranch for hogs and cattle and work through the night at their horse-powered flour mill to prepare life-saving seed grain and salted Traverse Bay whitefish for the desperate Selkirk Settlers.

name: Bas de la Rivière (Pine Falls, Fort Alexander)
original_story: The Whitefish Run: A vibrant seasonal story centered on the annual October migration of whitefish. The plot follows families as they gather on the gravel shoals of Traverse Bay, utilizing traditional fishing techniques to stock their winter larders. The narrative contrasts the tranquility of the river life with the frantic labor of the "Harvest Brigade" that once defined the post's importance to the entire northwest.

name: Batoche
original_story: Relocation West: A story follows a family from the Red River valley making the long journey west to their wintering grounds in Batoche to escape the social and political changes following 1870.

name: Battle River Valley
original_story: The Western Hunt: A narrative following a hunt brigade as they move through the Battle River Valley, documenting the seasonal lifestyle and kinship ties established in the western interior.

name: Berens River
original_story: The Poisoned Lake: A poignant story about a legendary fisherman from Berens River dealing with the sudden loss of his livelihood and his traditional connection to the water after the 1970 mercury crisis.

name: Big Eddy (Settlement)
original_story: Outside the Line: A political drama following a community leader in Big Eddy as he petitions the government to extend simple town services to his community, which is visible but legally excluded from the town's prosperity.

name: Birds Hill
original_story: Refuge in the Pines: A survival story set during the 1852 flood, where diverse groups of Métis and settlers huddle together on the high ground while the valley below becomes an inland sea.

name: Birsay Village (Orkney Town)
original_story: The Orkney Dream: A historical tragedy detailing the resilience and heartbreak of the Orkney freemen as they attempt to build a permanent home only to be defeated by disease and insects in 1819.

name: Black Butte (Butte Noir)
original_story: The Lookout: A story follows a buffalo scout stationed at Black Butte, monitoring the herds and approaching brigades during the peak of the 19th-century hunts.

name: Blood River Post (Bloodvein River)
original_story: The Blood River Brigade: A story following a York boat brigade's stop at the post, highlighting the cultural interactions between traders and local bands along the eastern shore.

name: Blueberry Patch
original_story: The Blue Harvest: A seasonal narrative focusing on the annual gathering of families at the patch, where traditional harvesting is blended with the sharing of oral histories and medicinal knowledge.

name: Boggy Creek and San Clara
original_story: The Pasture War: A narrative focusing on the 1930s displacement, following a family as they watch their homestead being transformed into a government-run pasture while their homes are burned.

name: Brandon House and Fort Assiniboine
original_story: The Capture of Brandon House: A tense historical drama set on June 1, 1816, following the strategic decisions of Cuthbert Grant's party as they seize the fort's pemmican stores to bypass the Red River colony.

name: Buffalo Lake
original_story: Winter at Buffalo Lake: A narrative detailing the social life and survival strategies of a Métis wintering brigade (hivernants) at Buffalo Lake during the mid-19th century.

name: Butte St. Paul ("Ot Accowabiwinins")
original_story: The Vision at the Butte: A story explores the traditional spiritual significance of the butte for the Métis and their ancestors who used it as a landmark and ceremonial site.

name: Calf Mountain (Tete de Boeuf), Little Dance Hill
original_story: The Ancestors' Smudge: A poignant modern story following Métis and Dakota descendants from Belcourt, North Dakota, as they make their annual autumn trek to the hill. The narrative focuses on the solemn ceremony of walking the mound, scattering tobacco, and offering food to the spirits of the warriors buried there, attempting to reclaim the spiritual significance of a site once desecrated by early 20th-century anthropologists.

name: Camperville
original_story: The Salt Makers: A story of the community's origins in the 1860s salt trade, focusing on families building a new life and the memory of the "salt cribs" at Flett's Point.

name: Carlton Trail (Métis Stopping Points)
original_story: The Freight Road: A narrative following a Red River cart brigade along the trail, highlighting the unique culture and challenges found at each stopping house during the long trek to Edmonton.

name: Carrot River Valley (Pasquia Settlement)
original_story: Pasquia Shadows: A narrative exploring early 20th-century farming efforts as Métis families navigated the transition from the fur trade to a sedentary life in the valley.

name: Catfish Creek
original_story: The Strategic Turn: An action-focused historical piece about Grant’s maneuver at Catfish Creek to bypass the HBC monopoly and prepare for the battle at Seven Oaks.

name: Cayer
original_story: The Settlement of Cayer: A story focused on 1950s life in Cayer, highlighting the social fabric and independence of this self-sufficient northern community.

name: Cedar Lake (Chemanwawin) Métis Settlement
original_story: The Sunken Village: A heartbreaking narrative following a Métis Elder who returns to the shore of the hydro reservoir. He watches the water where his home and the sturgeon fishery once existed, reflecting on the 1962 relocation where his family, who did not speak English, was pressured into signing documents they didn't understand, leading to the construction of permanent government dependency.

name: Charleswood (St. Charles Parish)
original_story: Bypassing the Forks: A story about independent traders in the 1840s using "The Passage" in Charleswood to bypass the HBC monopoly at the Forks.

name: Choteau
original_story: Frontier Ties: A story explores the kinship links between the Red River Métis and the Montana settlements like Choteau during the late 19th-century migrations.

name: Cold Lake
original_story: The Guiding Ghost: A story based on James Sayese, who guided the discovery of the Sherridon Mine but lived in a shack on the road allowance because he wasn't allowed to own land.

name: Cold Lake (Kississing)
original_story: The Guiding Ghost: A story based on James Sayese, who guided the discovery of the Sherridon Mine but lived in a shack on the road allowance because he wasn't allowed to own land.

name: Cooks Creek
original_story: Peguis’ Sanctuary: A narrative about the collaboration between Chief Peguis and Anglican missionaries to build a farming sanctuary for his people at Cooks Creek.

name: Cormorant
original_story: The Summerberry Trappers: A narrative centered on the seasonal trapping cycle in the marsh, seen through the eyes of a Cormorant family during the peak years of the northern trade.

name: Cranberry Portage
original_story: The Billion-Dollar Claim: A historical account following David Collins, exploring the irony of a trapper discovering a massive mining complex while receiving only a pittance in return.

name: Crane River (O-Chi-Chak-Ko-Sipi)
original_story: The Salt Expedition: A story of traditional salt-harvesting trips to Crane River, highlighting the shared labor and community ties of local families.

name: Crescentwood
original_story: The Advancing Suburb: A historical drama about a Métis family watching the surveyor’s stakes for the new suburb advance toward their river lot, signaling the end of their way of life.

name: Cumberland House
original_story: Gatekeeper of the North: A story about life at Cumberland House as a central hub where brigades and prospectors met guides like James Sayese.

name: Curling River Post
original_story: Fidler’s Stand: A rivalry story between the young Charles Fidler and the NWC traders at the nearby Desjarlais House during the height of the fur trade wars.

name: Cypress Hills
original_story: The Winter Brigade: A narrative detailing the lives of Métis families like the Dubois or Bonneaus during their wintering seasons in the Cypress Hills.

name: Deer Rapids
original_story: Limestone Sanctuary: A story following a family choosing the safety of Deer Rapids' high ground during a major flood year while central settlements are swept away.

name: Dog Head Settlement
original_story: Simard’s Claim: A narrative following a northern prospector as he travels the rugged shores of Lake Winnipeg, searching for gold while maintaining his connection to his community.

name: Dog Patch
original_story: Shanties by the Tracks: A narrative exploring the daily life and survival strategies of the "Dog Patch" community on the edge of the expanding industrial rail yards.

name: Duck Bay (Baie de Canard)
original_story: The Scrip Petition: A historical legal drama about the 1893 petition of the Duck Bay Métis as they struggle to reclaim their rights after being misled into taking scrip.

name: Duck Mountain
original_story: Forest Harvest: A story about the families who marketed furs hunted in the Duck Mountains via the Pelly Trail and Shell River.

name: Easterville
original_story: Resilience at Easterville: A modern story following a younger generation as they rediscover the traditional fishing skills of their Cedar Lake ancestors in a new land.

name: Eddystone
original_story: The Church of Lonely Lake: A story of community labor and faith as families build their own sanctuary during the height of the Great Depression.

name: Edmonton House (Fort Augustus)
original_story: The Long Trail: A story following a Red River cart brigade from St. Andrews to Edmonton as families seek new farmland and opportunities in the west.

name: Fairford Settlement (formerly Pinaymootang)
original_story: The Tense Trade: A narrative about the "fighting trade" of 1796 at Fairford, focusing on the fierce competition between independent traders and companies.

name: Fish Creek
original_story: The Strategic Turn: An action-focused historical piece about Grant’s maneuver at Catfish Creek to bypass the HBC monopoly and prepare for the battle at Seven Oaks.

name: Flee Island Entrenchments
original_story: The Ring of Pits: A survival story follows a Dakota family taking refuge in the trenches while being hunted after the 1862 uprising.

name: Fort Alexander (Sagkeeng)
original_story: The Pemmican Depot: A narrative about the strategic importance of Fort Alexander as the "gas station" of the northern canoe routes.

name: Fort Dauphin
original_story: Fidler’s Final Map: A historical piece focusing on the final days of Peter Fidler at Fort Dauphin, reflecting on his decades of exploration.

name: Fort de la Rivière Tremblante (Grant’s House, Aspin House)
original_story: Grant’s Favorite Post: A historical drama set during the winter of 1794. The plot focuses on the domestic life of Cuthbert Grant Sr. as he manages the logistics of a major provisioning post while navigating the tensions of the "fur trade wars." The story culminates in his decision to abandon his beloved fort as the market becomes overcrowded with competitors, signaling the end of an era on the Upper Assiniboine.

name: Fort Desjarlais
original_story: The Independent Trader: A historical thriller centered on the 1846 seizure of furs at Fort Desjarlais by Cuthbert Grant during a crackdown on illicit trade.

name: Fort Ellice
original_story: The McKay Dynasty: A narrative focused on the John Richard McKay family at Fort Ellice, depicting their role as powerful intermediaries.

name: Fort Gibralter
original_story: The Assembly at the Forks: A political drama set inside the fort in 1870 as the provisional government debates joining Canada.

name: Fort la Reine (Portage la Prairie)
original_story: Tanner’s Cart Train: A story about Peichito Tanner’s massive freighting operation moving goods across the plains.

name: Fort Langley
original_story: Coast to Coast: A narrative linking the plains Métis with their kin at Fort Langley, highlighting the vast reach of the family networks.

name: Fort Montagne à la Bosse
original_story: Harmon’s Enchanted Abode: A narrative about the "enchanted abode" described by Harmon where buffalo are always in view from the gate.

name: Fort Mr. Grant
original_story: Warden of the Plains: A standoff story set in 1824, where Cuthbert Grant uses his authority to protect his trade from American encroachment.

name: Fort Pelly
original_story: District Hub: A story follows a trader moving between Pelly and the surrounding outposts, documenting the logistical challenges of the Swan River trade.

name: Fort Pinancewaywining
original_story: First Cart from Pembina: A story about the very first cart caravan to arrive, marking the birth of overland transportation in the region.

name: Fort Rouge
original_story: The Lost River Lots: A historical mystery exploring how the original Métis owners of Fort Rouge were systematically displaced by land speculators.

name: Fort Souris
original_story: The Mandan Trade: A story about the competitive race in 1793 to reach the lucrative Mandan trade before the HBC.

name: Fort St. Charles
original_story: Discovery of the Ruins: A historical account following the 1902 discovery of the fort and the return of remains to St. Boniface.

name: Fouillard Town (The Corner / Li Kwayn)
original_story: Salvaged Timber: A story of profound spiritual and physical resilience. As the families of Ste. Madeleine watch their village burn, they decide to save what they can. The plot follows a group of men as they physically dismantle logs from their original church to drag them to "The Corner" to rebuild the St. Hubert Church, ensuring that their faith and community identity survive the displacement.

name: Four Persons
original_story: Montana Refuge: A story explores the lives of the "landless" Métis in communities like Four Persons after the migrations from Red River.

name: Frog Plain (La Grenouillère)
original_story: The Annual Rendezvous: A story of the grand gathering at Frog Plain, capturing the music, dance, and commerce of voyageur brigades.

name: Gabriel's Crossing
original_story: The Ferryman of the South: A narrative centered on Gabriel Dumont, depicting his role as a community leader and the logistics of operating the crossing.

name: Grand Marais
original_story: Memories of the Fiddle: A nostalgic story told by an Elder about the final years of the community's exclusive identity before the resort arrived.

name: Grand Rapids (Red River and Saskatchewan River)
original_story: Bulldozers at Dawn: A poignant narrative following a Métis family at the mouth of the Saskatchewan River. They are among the families whose homes were designated for destruction by the Grand Rapids Hydro Project. The story details the pressure they felt to sign away their land for "minimum sums" and the tragic day they watched their ancestral homes being leveled to make way for the dam.

name: Grande Pointe
original_story: The Railroad Laborers: A story about the Métis of Grande Pointe who built the very railroad that would eventually transform their community.

name: Grassy Narrows House
original_story: Winter at Grassy Narrows: A story set at the isolated outpost follows a Métis clerk managing the post during a harsh Lake Winnipeg winter.

name: Green Lake (Lac Vert)
original_story: The Northern Trade: A story about the canoe routes through Green Lake, depicting the life of the northern voyageurs.

name: Hair Hills or Pembina Mountains
original_story: The Wintering Band: A story set in the 1840s focusing on the life of a buffalo-hunting band surviving the cold in the shelter of the hills.

name: Havre
original_story: Across the 49th: A narrative exploring the cross-border migrations and scrip applications of the Havre Métis.

name: Heart Butte
original_story: Prairie Kinship: A story about the cultural and family ties between Heart Butte and the Canadian Red River Métis.

name: Îlets de Bois (now Carman)
original_story: The Sugar Moon: A seasonal story of the trek from Grantown to the maple groves where families find their kettles hidden exactly where they left them.

name: Island Cache
original_story: Mountain Crossing: A story follows a Métis family moving through the western passes to settle at Island Cache, linking the plains and the coast.

name: Jack River Post
original_story: Desperate Haven: A story of the 1815 evacuation of the colony to Jack River, highlighting the survival skills of Métis guides.

name: Kansas Half Breed Tracts
original_story: The US Reserve: A legal drama explores the struggle of families to hold onto land in the Kansas tracts during American westward expansion.

name: Kelly Lake
original_story: High Altitude Kinship: A story detailing the unique mountain lifestyle and enduring cultural traditions of the Kelly Lake Métis.

name: Kettle Stones
original_story: The Stone Medicine: A spiritual narrative exploring traditional significance and "medicine stories" associated with the Kettle Stones.

name: Kinosota
original_story: The Narrows Treaty: A historical drama focused on the signing of Treaty Two, depicting varying expectations of Métis and government.

name: La Chevulure Montagne Post (Hair Hills Post)
original_story: The Hill Trade: A narrative follows a trader managing the frantic trade during a peak buffalo season in the hills.

name: Laboucane (Duhamel)
original_story: Relocation to Duhamel: A story detailing the migration of the Laboucane family from Red River to build a new agricultural community in the west.

name: Lac des Chênes (Flat Creek)
original_story: Wintering at the Lake: A winter narrative focusing on the transition from temporary buffalo camp to permanent settlement at Oak Lake.

name: Lac Ste. Anne
original_story: Pilgrimage to the Lake: A story explores the spiritual traditions and annual summer gatherings at Lac Ste. Anne.

name: Lake Metigoshe Métis Settlement
original_story: Cross-Border Winter: A story about families whose lives spanned the international boundary before it was strictly enforced.

name: Lake Pepin
original_story: Mississippi Kin: A story follows families moving along the Mississippi trade routes between Red River and Lake Pepin.

name: Lane’s Post (St. François Xavier)
original_story: The Drugged Wine Plot: A suspenseful historical thriller set in January 1864. The story follows William Lane and John McKenzie as they coordinate a clandestine operation to capture the Dakota leaders. The narrative explores the ethics of the betrayal as they use the sanctuary of the post to offer drugged wine to the refugees, leading to their capture and delivery to American authorities at the border.

name: Last Mountain Lake
original_story: The High Ground Hunt: A narrative following a hunt brigade as they camp at Last Mountain Lake during a summer buffalo run.

name: Lido Plage
original_story: The Old Timers Picnic: A vibrant story set during a 1940s picnic, capturing the music and dance of a major Métis cultural celebration.

name: Little Britain
original_story: The Master Masons: A historical piece about stonemasons as they construct the Little Britain church using local limestone.

name: Little Chicago (Chicago Line)
original_story: Life on the Line: A story about the struggle for basic services in a community designated with a pejorative name by the nearby township.

name: Little Grand Rapids House
original_story: The Last York Boat: A story about the final boat trip to the post in 1871, marking the end of a legendary era of transportation.

name: Little Saskatchewan River
original_story: Rapide Waters: A narrative centered on the ancient buffalo migration routes through the river valley as seen by a Métis hunting party.

name: Longbody Creek (Kenepikiniwewe)
original_story: The Short-Lived Home: A modern story about the intense sense of community at Longbody Creek and the eventual return of its residents.

name: Loon Straits
original_story: The Master Boatbuilder: A story of a craftsman at Loon Straits whose custom-built boat survives a legendary storm that sinks larger vessels.

name: Lorette (Petit Point des Chênes)
original_story:
Grazing the Seine: A story of founding families searching for new grazing lands and the eventual renaming of the parish by Bishop Tache.

---

Graze and Freight: A story follows founding families as they graze cattle along the Seine and operate freighting brigades.

name: MacGregor
original_story: Lobbying for Land: A story about the leadership of Alfred Roulette in 1961 as he lobbies the municipality for housing for the landless Métis.

name: Manigotagan (Bad Throat River)
original_story: Sound of the Throat: A narrative exploring the legend of the eerie sound at Wood Falls, set against the 1904 river lot survey.

name: Manitoba House (Kinosota)
original_story: Post at the Narrows: A historical story about daily operations seen through the eyes of its Métis staff.

name: Mapleton
original_story: Old England on the Red: A story about the cultural blend of Orkney-descended families and Saulteaux people in the district.

name: Matheson Island (formerly Snake Island)
original_story: The Island Keeper: A story centered on Daniel Matheson, the first lighthouse keeper, watching lake traffic from his island home.

name: Meadow Portage
original_story: The Plain Portage: A story about the strategic route used for centuries to move between the great lakes of Manitoba.

name: Metikewap (Wooden/Bark Tent)
original_story: The Bark Tents: A story about the final season at Metikewap as residents prepare to leave before the rising water arrives.

name: Métis Island
original_story: Moving the Camp: A narrative about relocation from the island to the permanent settlement on the riverbank.

name: Michipicoten Post
original_story: Superior Trade: A story detailing the importance of Michipicoten as a transshipment point for Red River goods moving toward the east.

name: Milk River
original_story: Expulsion from the Plains: A story follows a family during the 1882 expulsion from Montana back across the border into Canada.

name: Moose Lake (Settlement)
original_story: The Flooded Delta: A narrative focused on the Summerberry Marsh trappers dealing with the loss of their traditional lands.

name: Netley Creek (Rivière aux Morts)
original_story: The Netley Gardens: A story set in 1805 depicting thriving gardens and traders arriving to buy produce.

name: Norway House and Rossville
original_story: Council of Factors: A story about the grand meetings at Norway House, depicting its role as the fur trade capital.

name: Oak Lake (Lac des Chênes / Métis Wintering Sites)
original_story: Wintering at the Lake: A winter narrative focusing on the 1860s transition from seasonal camp to established settlement.

name: Oak Point House and Oak Point
original_story: The Oak Point Nation: A story about the signing of Treaty One and the pressure put on the local band to relocate.

name: Opaskweyaw (The Pas)
original_story: Devon Mission: A biographical story about Rev. Henry Budd, exploring his leadership in building the local mission.

name: Oxford House
original_story: The Sinclair Family: A family saga following the Sinclairs, depicting life on the northern trade route.

name: Park Valley
original_story: Valley Life: A narrative detailing the seasonal patterns of harvesting and kinship in Park Valley during the settlement era.

name: Pembina
original_story: The Wintering Band: A story set in the 1840s focusing on the life of a buffalo-hunting band surviving the cold in the shelter of the hills.

name: Petit Point de Roches
original_story: Freighters on the Red: A story about the freighting life following a cart train stopping at the river lots.

name: Pikwitonei
original_story: End of the Steel: A story about Luke Clemons, a Métis man managing transportation at the terminus using a gas car.

name: Pine Bluff
original_story: The Vanishing Home: A legal drama about families losing their homes to a dam while being told they have no legal right to compensation for unsurveyed land.

name: Pine Dock (Bullhead)
original_story: The Bullhead Fishery: A story follows Jesse Matheson operating a stopping place for lake travelers during the York boat era.

name: Fort des Épinettes (Pine Fort)
original_story: Trade with the Mandans: A story detailing a 1790 horse-back mission from Pine Fort to Mandan villages across the plains.

name: Pointe à Grouette (Petite Pointe à Saline, St. Agathe)
original_story: Disenfranchised Lands: A drama about a family in 1870 losing their land due to mismanagement of the scrip system.

name: Pointe Coupée (St. Adolphe)
original_story: The St. Adolphe Resistance: An action story about the day Métis pulled out John Snow’s survey stakes to protect their property.

name: Pointe des Chênes (Grande Pointe des Chênes, Ste. Anne de Chênes)
original_story: The Road Builders: A story about Métis laborers building the Dawson Road while their own land rights are ignored by surveyors.

name: Pointe du Chien-Maigre
original_story: Post on the Plain: A narrative detailing life at the Carlton House post under its traditional name, focusing on the buffalo trade.

name: Poitras House
original_story: Trader Poitras: A narrative following the trader managing complex relations on the frontier Assiniboine.

name: Poplar Point
original_story: The Church Bell’s Journey: A narrative about Poplar Point settlers building their church and installing its famous historic bell.

name: Portage La Loche
original_story: The Long Portage: A story follows the Portage La Loche brigade, capturing the extreme physical labor and camaraderie of the northern boatmen.

name: Prairie à Fournier
original_story: School on the Prairie: A story follows the Nolin sisters establishing a four-language school at the mission for local children.

name: Rabbit Point (Princess Harbour)
original_story: Princess of the Harbour: A story of the community's unique identity and life as a remote northern fishing settlement.

name: Rall’s (Rahls) Island (Settlement)
original_story: Charles Rall’s Home: A narrative follows a prospector who decides to build a homestead for his family in the northern delta.

name: Rat Portage
original_story: Boundary Wars: A political drama about the confusion when the town was incorporated by both provinces simultaneously in 1882.

name: Rat River (Rivière aux Rats)
original_story: Rat River Winterers: A narrative about the 1825 petition of the local Métis for permanent land title near the salt works.

name: Red Deer Lake
original_story: The Last Sawmill: A historical story set in the late 1920s detailing the sudden collapse of the local lumber industry. A family must weigh their deep ties to the forest against the economic pressure to move south to the city.

name: Red River Settlements (North of Upper Fort Garry)
original_story: Generations on the Red: A family saga following a Métis family on their narrow lot from the fur trade through the 1869 Resistance.

name: Riding Mountain
original_story: Hunts of the Mountain: A spiritual story following a hunter through the sacred migration routes and the Elk Sacred Grounds.

name: Rivière Esturgeon (St. Charles)
original_story: Crossing the Esturgeon: A story set during the 1852 flood as families flee to the higher ground of the St. Charles parish.

name: Rivière Gratchias (Morris)
original_story: Cart Trains at Gratchias: A narrative exploring the vibrant culture of cart trains as they rest and repair at the Morris site.

name: Rivière Sale (St. Norbert)
original_story: The Barrière Morning: A historical drama centered on the morning the Métis blockade turned back the Canadian governor and his retinue.

name: Rock Lake (Lac des Roches)
original_story: The Lake Band: A story about intermarried families depicting their shared life and trade connections in the Pembina hills.

name: Rooster Town (Pakan Town)
original_story: Hazelnut Summer: A poignant story follows a child harvesting nuts while sensing the impending arrival of the city’s bulldozers in 1959.

name: Salt Point Métis Settlement
original_story: The Brine Boilers: A story detailing the grueling labor of the Monkman family as they harvest and evaporate salt across the lake from Winnipegosis to supply the northern brigades.

name: Sandy Bay
original_story: The Scrip Deception: A legal drama following François Desmerais and the 1886 petition to the Prime Minister, detailing how families felt "forced" to take scrip and sell it for goods, only to regret it a week later.

name: Sault à la Biche (Deer Rapids, St. Andrews)
original_story: Refuge on the Rapids: A historical survival story following a family as they flee the 1826 flood, finding safety on the limestone banks of Sault à la Biche while the central settlement is submerged.

name: Seven Oaks (Sept Chênes)
original_story: Paashkiiyaakanaan (We Won): An action-filled historical retelling of June 19, 1816, following the young Joseph Letendre dit Batoche during the frantic minutes of the battle that changed the course of the fur trade.

name: Shoal River (Shoal River House)
original_story: Trade at Shoal River: A narrative focused on the seasonal interactions and kinship ties between the Métis traders at the post and the members of the Shoal River Cree-Saulteaux Band.

name: Skownan, Rock Ridge and Mallard Métis Settlements
original_story:
Turning the Point: A family saga following the migration of families between Mallard and Rock Ridge in 1895, drawn by the abundance of wild game, berries, and Seneca root.

---

The Enniship Settlers: A pioneer story about families who moved to Mallard in 1895, drawn by traditional harvesting opportunities.

name: Smokey River
original_story: Scrip on the Smokey: A narrative exploring the 1885 scrip commission's visit to the Smokey River area. The story follows a family as they weigh the government's offer of scrip against their traditional land rights, illustrating the legal and social shifts occurring in the western Métis settlements during the late 19th century.

name: South Indian Lake Métis Settlement
original_story: The Diverted River: A modern tragedy following a commercial fisherman at South Indian Lake as he watches the rising, murky waters of the hydro project destroy the pristine whitefish grounds of his ancestors.

name: Spring Creek (Lewistown)
original_story: Montana Pioneers: A family saga detailing the long trek from the Red River valley to Spring Creek, Montana. The plot explores how the community established a new life on the "Front Range," maintaining their Michif language and Catholic traditions in a new territory.

name: St. Ambroise
original_story: Berry Summer, Fish Fall: A vibrant seasonal narrative exploring the life of the community as they transition from the July berry festival to the intense labor of the fall whitefish spawn.

name: St. Ambroise Dakota Entrenchment (Historic Site)
original_story: Rifle Pits at Dawn: A tense historical drama set in May 1864, as the Dakota frantically dig entrenchments to protect their women and children from a looming attack by Minnesota bounty hunters.

name: St. Andrews
original_story: Refuge on the Rapids: A historical survival story following a family as they flee the 1826 flood, finding safety on the limestone banks of Sault à la Biche while the central settlement is submerged.

name: St. Boniface
original_story:
The Heart of the Nation: A historical account of the founding of the mission and its transformation into the central gathering place for the legislative assembly that brought Manitoba into Confederation.

---

The Forks of History: A sweeping historical narrative set during the winter of 1869-70. The plot follows the families of St. Boniface as they gather at the Cathedral and Upper Fort Garry to support Louis Riel’s provisional government, illustrating the community's role in the birth of the province of Manitoba and the defense of Métis rights.

name: St. Eustache (formerly Baie St. Paul)
original_story: The Saint of Hunters: A historical story about the community's rebirth in 1898. Following the heartbreak of seeing their ancestors' graves slide into the Assiniboine River in 1882, the narrative follows a family as they work with missionary Thomas Quevillion to build a new church on safer ground, dedicating their new home to St. Eustache to honor their enduring identity as buffalo hunters.

name: St. François Xavier
original_story: The Cart Builders: An epic narrative about the assembly of a 900-person cart brigade at Grantown for the massive annual hunt on the plains.

name: St. Joseph (Walhalla)
original_story: The Mission on the Hill: A story focused on the 1853 relocation of Belcourt’s parishioners from Baie St. Paul to St. Joseph. The plot details the labor of building a new school and church on the slopes of the Pembina Hills, creating a sanctuary for families seeking a life away from the fur trade monopoly of the Hudson's Bay Company.

name: St. Laurent
original_story: Persistence of Michif: A linguistic drama set in a schoolroom where Métis students secretly maintain their language despite authorities' pressure to change.

name: St. Mary’s (Portage la Prairie)
original_story: Portage Bound: A narrative following the Anderson and Whitford families as they drive their carts west from the crowded Red River to stake a new future at St. Mary’s.

name: St. Peter's Mission
original_story: Riel’s Classmates: A narrative set in the mission schoolroom, exploring the intellectual and political awakening of young Métis students. The story details the influence of the mission's teachings on their understanding of land rights and national identity, which would later fuel the Resistance movements.

name: St. Pierre
original_story: Naming the Waters: A narrative following explorer's men as they name the winding rivers in the 1730s.

name: St. Vital
original_story: Raising Riel: A domestic drama set in the Riel home in St. Vital, following the young Louis as he learns the traditions of the buffalo hunt and the responsibilities of leadership.

name: Ste. Madeleine
original_story: Defending the Bell: An intense story of defiance where Joe Venne and community members arm themselves with rifles to confront the crew sent to dismantle their church, saving the building from becoming a piggery.

name: Ste. Rose du Lac
original_story: The Exploratory Trip: A story following the Neault and Spence families on their 190-mile journey from St. Vital to the Turtle River, discovering graves marked by a tall oak cross that proved their ancestors were already there.

name: Stony Mountain
original_story: Floods of the Valley: A story follows a cart brigade during the early 1800s, racing against rising waters to reach the safety of the Stony Mountain plateau.

name: Stovepipe (Tuyau)
original_story: Shanties of Stovepipe: A survival story detailing the daily life of a woodcutter's family in the Ste. Anne area, living in a road allowance camp during a bitter winter.

name: Sugar Island (Lake Manitoba)
original_story: Syrup Spring: A cultural narrative centered on Nonoonse as she returns to Sugar Island to tap the maples, demonstrating the enduring spiritual and economic importance of the sugar bush.

name: Summerberry Marsh
original_story: The Muskrat Marsh: A family narrative following the impact of the Grand Rapids Generating Station on commercial trapping and the eventual fight for historical justice and compensation.

name: Swan Creek
original_story: The Monkmans of Swan: A story following a family moving from their isolated creek-side home to the growing community of St. Laurent following the 1871 treaty.

name: Swan River (and Swan River House)
original_story: Bark and Fur: A historical piece focusing on the skilled Métis canoe-makers at Swan River House who were vital to the logistics of the entire northern trade network.

name: Tanner’s Crossing (Minnedosa)
original_story: The Crossing Ferry: A historical piece following John Tanner’s journey from the Union Army to the Little Saskatchewan River, establishing a vital hub for the western cart trains.

name: Teton River
original_story: The South Fork Hunt: A narrative following a Métis hunting brigade as they establish a winter camp on the Teton River. The story illustrates the vast geographic reach of the Métis family networks, linking the Montana plains to the Red River settlements through marriage and trade.

name: The Pas
original_story: Devon Mission: A biographical story about Rev. Henry Budd, exploring his leadership in building the local mission.

name: The Passage (Charleswood)
original_story: Bypassing the Forks: A suspenseful story about "free traders" in the 1840s using "The Passage" under the cover of night to smuggle furs past the company's eyes.

name: Thicket Portage
original_story: Fishing the Fiddle: A narrative detailing the seasonal rhythms of the sturgeon fishery at Thicket Portage and the transition from river travel to the "Bay Line" railway era.

name: Thomas Métis Settlement
original_story:
The Thomas Claim: A story of the family's struggle for land title and recognition as they lobby the government to survey the Crown lands they called home.

---

The Sarcastic Name: A story of community resilience where the displaced families of Ste. Madeleine use humor as a weapon of protest, naming their shanty town after the man who signed the order to burn their homes.

name: Tin Town
original_story: Baseball on the Flats: A lighthearted sports story about high-stakes baseball games played on dirt lots between the children of Rooster Town and Tin Town in the 1950s.

name: Tokyo at Crescent Lake
original_story: Life at Crescent Lake: A communal story detailing the daily life of families in "Tokyo," exploring their strategies for self-sufficiency through small-scale farming and trapping while maintaining their unique Michif culture in the Saskatchewan interior.

name: Turtle Mountain (and Turtle Mountain House)
original_story: Starvation Winter: A historical drama focused on the 1894 winter, depicting the community's struggle for survival and the failure of government aid during an extreme blizzard.

name: Umphreville or Humphreville (Settlement)
original_story: The Umphreville Saga: A story following the patriarch’s descendants as they build a legacy in the northern river delta despite being excluded from town services.

name: Upper Athabasca
original_story: The Mountain Voyageurs: A historical piece following the descendants of Iroquois "trip-men" as they settle in the Upper Athabasca. The narrative explores the blending of cultures in the mountain foothills, creating a distinct Métis identity rooted in the demanding labor of the northwestern canoe brigades.

name: Upper Fort Garry
original_story: Generations on the Red: A family saga following a Métis family on their narrow lot from the fur trade through the 1869 Resistance.

name: Wabowden
original_story: Fish Station Rhythms: A narrative centered on the Wabowden fish station, exploring the logistics and camaraderie of the northern commercial fishery.

name: Waterhen River (Rivière de la Poule d'Eau)
original_story: Traversing the Waterhen: A travel narrative following a hunting party as they navigate the complex river channels between the two great Manitoba lakes.

name: Wekusko (Herb) Lake
original_story: Herb Lake Gold: A gold rush story from a Métis perspective, depicting the impact of the sudden influx of prospectors on traditional trapping grounds.

name: Wheat City
original_story: Old Mrs. Morrissette’s Memory: A narrative centered on "Old Mrs. Morrissette" recounting her vivid memories of the 1826 flood for the children of the 1880s settlers.

name: Whiskey Flats
original_story: Life on the Flats: A survival story set in a road allowance community, where families use their ingenuity to build homes from scrap materials. The narrative focuses on the community's resilience in the face of social segregation and their determination to provide an education for their children despite being excluded from municipal services.

name: White Mud River Settlement (Rat Creek and Totogan)
original_story:
The White Mud Census: A story based on the 1870 census of the 150 residents, detailing the daily lives of hunters and their families before the 1881 floods.

---

The Chicago of the North: A historical tragedy depicting the final days of Totogan as floodwaters rise, causing buildings to float away and settlers to pull up stakes.

name: Whiteway Point
original_story: Post at the Narrows: A historical account of daily life at the isolated Lake Winnipeg outpost as seen through the eyes of its Métis staff.

name: Williams County
original_story: Borderland Kin: A story of a Métis family whose land holdings and kinship ties span the boundary between Manitoba and Williams County. The plot centers on the challenges of navigating new international regulations that threatened to divide their community and their traditional hunting territories.

name: Willow Bunch
original_story: The Willow Haven: A narrative set after the 1885 Resistance, following families as they find sanctuary in Willow Bunch. The story details the community's transformation from a temporary wintering site into a permanent cultural hub for the plains Métis.

name: Willow Flats
original_story: Northern Resilience: A modern story set in the NWT, focusing on the history of the Willow Flats residents as they advocate for land titles and recognition of their unique contributions to the northern economy and transportation networks.

name: Winnipegosis
original_story: Little Muddy Water Boom: A story set during the community's economic peak, following a family working in the timber mills during the early 1900s.

name: York Factory
original_story: The Star Fort: A historical piece detailing the 1697 Battle of Hudson’s Bay—the largest northern naval battle—taking place just off the coast of the factory.

name: Young Point Settlement
original_story: Outside the Boundaries: A social drama focusing on the Josiah Young family as they build a productive life on the road allowance while being denied town water and sewer.
